sa_value: overweight_at=23; obese_at=27.5
source_labels: WHO Expert Consultation — appropriate BMI for Asian populations (Lancet 2004)
overclaim_guard: Lower cutoffs reflect earlier metabolic risk, not a different disease; do not restate as 'South Asians are unhealthy at normal weight'.
notes: Encoded in loinc.yaml as PRAJNA-BMI ref_high 23. Source in evidence registry as who-asian-bmi-2004 (grade A, approved). obese_at corrected 25->27.5 (WHO 2004 Asian high-risk action point; 2026-07-11).

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=23
source_labels: Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2024 | Optimum BMI cut points to screen Asian Americans for type 2 diabetes (Araneta/Kanaya, Diabetes Care 2015)
overclaim_guard: A screening trigger is not a diagnosis; a positive trigger means 'test glucose', not 'has diabetes'.
notes: SouthAsianScreeningPack.diabetesScreenBMI23 (v2024.2), fires from age 30.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=30
source_labels: ADA Standards of Care 2024 | Araneta/Kanaya, Diabetes Care 2015
notes: South Asians develop dysglycaemia ~a decade earlier; screen earlier.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=90
source_labels: IDF consensus worldwide definition of the metabolic syndrome — ethnic-specific waist cutoffs
notes: loinc.yaml 56115-9 ref_high 90 encodes the male SA cut.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=80
source_labels: IDF consensus worldwide definition of the metabolic syndrome
notes: SouthAsianScreeningPack.isCentralObesity female cut = 80 cm.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=0.5
source_labels: Comparison of obesity indices for CVD risk classification in South Asian adults — The CARRS Study (Patel et al., PLoS One 2017)
overclaim_guard: WtHR >=0.5 is a general adiposity rule; the South-Asian-specific claim is that it outperforms BMI here, not a distinct cutoff.
notes: Used in SouthAsianScreeningPack.isCentralObesity as a fallback when waist+height on file. Source in evidence registry as carrs-patel-2017 (review_status: proposed — awaiting clinician sign-off).

sa_value: comparator=<; value=50
source_labels: IDF metabolic syndrome consensus (2006) | Prevention of ASCVD in South Asians — NLA clinical perspective (2021)
notes: IDF sex-specific low-HDL cut; women flagged at <50.

sa_value: comparator=<; value=40
source_labels: IDF metabolic syndrome consensus (2006)
overclaim_guard: Male cut equals the general threshold; the SA-specific piece is the female cut and the pattern's weight, not this value.
notes: Included for completeness so the male/female contrast is explicit.

sa_value: triglycerides_mg_dl=>=150; with_low_hdl=True
source_labels: AHA Scientific Statement — ASCVD in South Asians (Volgman 2018) | Lipid Association of India SA lipid targets (Enas 2013) | Risk factors for early MI in South Asians (INTERHEART, JAMA 2007)
overclaim_guard: A normal LDL-C does not rule out risk when TG is high and HDL low; do not present LDL-C as the sole lipid target for SA users.
notes: The SA-characteristic pattern (small-dense LDL, high ApoB/ApoA-I) that drives risk at normal LDL-C. Component values match general thresholds; the differentiation is the emphasis.

sa_value: low=60; high=90
source_labels: NLA — Prevention of ASCVD in South Asians (2021) | AHA Volgman 2018
overclaim_guard: The range is not SA-specific; the SA-specific claim is preference of ApoB over LDL-C, not a different cutoff.
notes: SouthAsianScreeningPack.apobPreferred nudges ApoB when a lipid panel exists but ApoB doesn't.

sa_value: elevated_at=50; test_frequency=once_in_adulthood
source_labels: NLA 2024 Lp(a) focused update / AHA 2024 Lp(a) toolkit | 2018 AHA/ACC/Multi-Society Blood Cholesterol Guideline
overclaim_guard: Do NOT claim South Asians universally run high Lp(a): MASALA (Huffman 2019) found no Lp(a)-atherosclerosis signal within the cohort. The rationale is 'genetically fixed + test-once is cheap', plus higher elevated-prevalence, not a proven SA outcome association.
notes: The 'test once' guidance is guideline-endorsed and not SA-specific; it appears here because it is under-done in this population. loinc.yaml Lp(a) mass ref_high 30 mg/dL, molar 43583-7 ref_high 75 nmol/L.

sa_value: high=130; note=LAI recommends more aggressive non-HDL targets by risk stratum
source_labels: Lipid Association of India SA lipid targets (Enas 2013)
overclaim_guard: LAI targets are risk-stratified; do not present a single non-HDL cutoff as applying to all SA users.
notes: Default non-HDL ref_high 130 from loinc.yaml; SA-specific aggressiveness is stratum-dependent — represented qualitatively pending clinician review.

sa_value: confirm_when=5.7-6.4; confirm_with=fasting glucose or OGTT
source_labels: Isolated HbA1c identifies a different T2D subgroup vs glucose in Asian Indians — CARRS & MASALA (Gujral 2019) | ADA Standards of Care 2024
overclaim_guard: HbA1c is not universally 'wrong' in SA users; the claim is that it can under- or over-read (iron deficiency, glycation rate), so borderline values warrant glucose confirmation.
notes: The reference range is not changed; this is an interpretation flag on borderline values.

analyte: Coronary artery calcium — baseline discussion age
sa_value: comparator=>=; value=35
source_labels: AHA Scientific Statement — ASCVD in South Asians (Volgman 2018)
notes: SA develop CAD ~10 years earlier; earlier baseline CAC discussion. In-app, CAC nudges tie to PREVENT 5-<20% band.

sa_value: low=30; high=100
source_labels: Endocrine Society — Evaluation, Treatment, and Prevention of Vitamin D Deficiency (2011)
overclaim_guard: The reference range is not SA-specific; the SA-relevant point is high deficiency prevalence, warranting screening — not a different normal range.
notes: SouthAsianScreeningPack.vitaminDScreen prompts if not measured within a year.

sa_value: low=2; high=15
source_labels: MASALA cardiometabolic profile / HOMA-IR vs MESA (Kanaya 2014)
overclaim_guard: Explicitly NOT guideline-backed; present as an optional/exploratory marker, never as recommended screening.
notes: In-app copy flags this as not guideline-backed. Good example of an honest study-derived row.

sa_value: adjust=False; guidance=do NOT apply a race/ancestry coefficient
source_labels: New Creatinine- and Cystatin C–Based Equations without Race (CKD-EPI 2021, Inker et al., NEJM)
overclaim_guard: Do NOT ship an ancestry-adjusted eGFR. The 2021 refit removed the race term as a social-not-biological construct that delayed care. This row exists to instruct consumers to NOT stratify eGFR by ancestry.
notes: The credibility showcase row: an adjustment the field is moving away from, included precisely to warn against it. Consistent with loinc.yaml keeping eGFR at real LOINC 33914-3 with no ancestry term.

sa_value: adjustment=none available; guidance=treat estimate as a floor; it underestimates SA risk
source_labels: Development and Validation of the AHA PREVENT Equations (Circulation 2024)
overclaim_guard: There is no validated SA multiplier in PREVENT; do not invent one. State the limitation ('treat as a floor'), do not apply a made-up adjustment.
notes: Contrast with the eGFR row: here we flag under-estimation but still refuse to fabricate an adjustment. loinc.yaml PRAJNA-PREVENT plain_language already says 'treat it as a floor'.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=175
source_labels: 2018 AHA/ACC/Multi-Society Blood Cholesterol Guideline
overclaim_guard: The 175 mg/dL cut is not SA-specific; the SA-relevant point is that atherogenic dyslipidemia (high TG + low HDL) reaches it often even at a normal LDL-C.
notes: Completes the atherogenic-dyslipidemia composite inputs. TG LOINC 2571-8.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=2
source_labels: 2018 AHA/ACC/Multi-Society Blood Cholesterol Guideline
overclaim_guard: The >=2 mg/L cut is not SA-specific; SA users more often exceed it due to higher visceral adiposity at the same BMI.
notes: hs-CRP LOINC 30522-7.

sa_value: ancestry_is_ascvd_risk_enhancer=True; guidance=The 2018 AHA/ACC guideline lists South Asian ancestry as a formal ASCVD risk-enhancing factor; use it to move borderline/intermediate-risk patients toward the treat decision.
source_labels: 2018 AHA/ACC/Multi-Society Blood Cholesterol Guideline
overclaim_guard: A risk-enhancer is not a risk multiplier; it does not by itself diagnose disease or mandate a statin — it shifts the borderline decision.
notes: Explicitly named in the 2018 AHA/ACC Cholesterol Guideline risk-enhancer list.

sa_value: ldl_c_target_very_high_risk=<70; non_hdl_target_very_high_risk=<100; guidance=LAI recommends more aggressive LDL-C/non-HDL targets for SA very-high-risk patients.
source_labels: Lipid Association of India SA lipid targets (Enas 2013) | NLA — Prevention of ASCVD in South Asians (2021)
overclaim_guard: LAI targets are risk-stratified; do not present a single cutoff as applying to all SA users. LDL-C <70 matches general very-high-risk guidance; the SA-specific piece is the tighter non-HDL target.
notes: Tightens the non-HDL-130 reference row for the very-high-risk stratum. review pending clinician sign-off.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=23
source_labels: Multi-society Delphi consensus statement on new fatty liver disease nomenclature (MASLD) (Rinella et al., 2023) | Ectopic fat depots and coronary artery calcium in South Asians vs other ethnic groups (Garg et al., JAHA 2016)
overclaim_guard: A lower screening BMI reflects earlier ectopic-fat accumulation, not a different disease; MASLD is diagnosed by imaging/enzymes, not by BMI alone.
notes: SA carry the highest intrahepatic fat of MESA ethnic groups at lower BMI (Garg 2016). MASLD nomenclature (2023) uses BMI >=23 for Asian populations.

sa_value: multiplier_indian_bangladeshi=1.3; multiplier_pakistani=1.7; guidance=Multiply the SCORE2 estimate by the ancestry-specific factor before applying risk thresholds.
source_labels: 2021 ESC Guidelines on cardiovascular disease prevention in clinical practice
overclaim_guard: The multiplier applies to SCORE2 specifically (derived from QRISK3 data); do not transplant it onto PREVENT or the Pooled Cohort Equations, which have no validated SA multiplier.
notes: The only major guideline with a numeric SA correction factor (2021 ESC, Class IIa LOE B). Companion to the PREVENT 'treat as a floor' caveat row.

analyte: Coronary artery calcium (South Asian age/sex percentiles)
sa_value: prevalence_cac_gt0={'age50': {'men': 0.45, 'women': 0.2}, 'age60': {'men': 0.7, 'women': 0.4}, 'age70': {'men': 0.9, 'women': 0.7}}; p75_age60={'men': 186, 'women': 26}; guidance=Use age/sex-specific percentiles, not the raw 100 cutoff, for younger SA patients.
source_labels: South Asian coronary artery calcium age/sex percentiles — MASALA + DILWALE (Tasdighi et al., JACC Advances 2025)
overclaim_guard: These are cohort percentiles (MASALA+DILWALE), not a guideline threshold; CAC=0 remains reassuring at age 50 (55% of men, 80% of women) but less so at 70.
notes: First SA-specific CAC percentile table to exist (n=2,743, ASCVD-free). review pending clinician sign-off.

sa_value: comparator=>=; value=33
source_labels: Remnant cholesterol and incident coronary artery calcium in South Asians — MASALA (Manghis et al., J Clin Lipidol 2025)
overclaim_guard: Explicitly not a guideline cutoff; the top MASALA quartile (>=~33 mg/dL) was associated with incident CAC, not CAC progression. Present as exploratory. Calculate as total cholesterol - HDL-C - LDL-C.
notes: MASALA: Q4 vs Q1 OR 2.57 for incident CAC over 5y, independent of LDL-C. review pending clinician sign-off.

sa_value: adjust=False; guidance=Do NOT apply APOL1 G1/G2 kidney-risk stratification to South Asians — these variants are high-frequency only in West-African ancestry (<1% in non-Africans).
source_labels: APOL1 kidney-risk variants are West-African ancestry–specific (review)
overclaim_guard: This row exists to instruct consumers NOT to use APOL1 in a South Asian risk model; including it would be a false-ancestry-attribution error.
notes: Credibility-showcase 'do-not-apply' row, in the spirit of the deprecated eGFR row.

sa_value: adjust=False; guidance=Do NOT surface alcohol-flush / fast-metabolizer warnings for South Asians based on ALDH2*2 or ADH1B*2 — these are predominantly East Asian (low single-digit % in SA).
source_labels: ALDH2*2 / ADH1B*2 alcohol-metabolism variants are predominantly East Asian (review)
overclaim_guard: This row exists to instruct consumers NOT to apply East-Asian alcohol-metabolism genetics to South Asians.
notes: Credibility-showcase 'do-not-apply' row. South Asians more often carry the slower ADH1B*1.

sa_value: low=200; high=900
source_labels: Hyperhomocysteinemia & cobalamin (B12) deficiency in Asian Indians (Refsum et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2001) | Vitamin B12 deficiency & hyperhomocysteinemia in rural and urban Indians (Yajnik et al., JAPI 2006)
overclaim_guard: The reference range is not SA-specific; the SA-relevant point is high deficiency prevalence in vegetarian South Asians, warranting screening — not a different normal range.
notes: Exact parallel to the vitamin-D screening-emphasis row. B12 LOINC 2132-9.

sa_value: mcv_below=80; confirm_with=hemoglobin electrophoresis / HbA2; guidance=Offer carrier screening in South Asian ancestry (higher β-thalassemia, HbE, HbD-Punjab prevalence), especially preconception; low MCV with normal iron warrants electrophoresis.
source_labels: ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 78: Hemoglobinopathies in Pregnancy (Obstet Gynecol 2007)
overclaim_guard: A carrier trait is not a disease; screening informs reproductive counseling and explains a spuriously altered HbA1c — it is not a diagnosis.
notes: Also the mechanism behind the HbA1c interpretation caveat. MCV LOINC 30428-7.

sa_value: guidance=A normal ALT does not exclude MASLD; in SA with metabolic risk, use imaging (ultrasound / hepatic-fat) rather than relying on enzymes.
source_labels: Proportion of NAFLD/MASLD patients with normal ALT: systematic review & meta-analysis (BMC Gastroenterol 2020)
overclaim_guard: ALT is not universally unreliable; the point is that a normal value is insufficient to rule out fatty liver in a high-metabolic-risk SA profile.
notes: Pairs with the BMI-23 MASLD screening trigger. ALT LOINC 1742-6.

sa_value: guidance=A mildly low WBC / absolute neutrophil count can be a normal ancestry-linked variant; do not over-investigate isolated mild neutropenia without other features.
source_labels: Benign ethnic neutropenia (Atallah-Yunes et al., Blood Rev 2019)
overclaim_guard: Benign ethnic neutropenia is best-established for African ancestry; the South Asian evidence base is thinner — flag it as a possibility, not a rule.
notes: Included with an explicit evidence-strength caveat. WBC LOINC 6690-2.

sa_value: variant=SLCO1B1 rs4149056 (c.521T>C); action=In decreased-function carriers, limit simvastatin dose and prefer rosuvastatin or pravastatin.; frequency_note=C-allele frequency in SA is broadly comparable to White populations (~15-20%).
source_labels: CPIC Guideline for SLCO1B1/ABCG2/CYP2C9 & statin-associated musculoskeletal symptoms (Clin Pharmacol Ther 2022)
overclaim_guard: The recommendation is genotype-guided statin selection, not SA-specific dosing; there is no SA-specific dosing table.
notes: CPIC 2022 statin/SLCO1B1 guideline. Default move in SA with statin myalgia: consider genotyping and switch off simvastatin before declaring intolerance.

sa_value: overweight_percentile=75; obese_percentile=95; adult_equivalent=overweight = adult BMI 23, obese = adult BMI 27 at age 18
source_labels: Revised IAP 2015 growth charts / BMI cut-offs for 5-18y Indian children (Khadilkar et al., Indian J Endocrinol Metab 2015)
overclaim_guard: These are cardiometabolic-risk-adjusted centiles on IAP 2015 charts; under-5 growth still follows WHO international standards regardless of ethnicity.
notes: Plot SA children on CDC and IAP charts; when CDC says normal but IAP says at-risk, treat as at-risk. IAP 2015 (Khadilkar).

analyte: Diabetes risk at low BMI (lean-diabetes phenotype)
sa_value: guidance=Do not gate diabetes-risk screening on BMI>=25 — T2D at BMI<25 is 2-4x more common in Asian Indians. Screen from BMI 23 and use waist-to-height >=0.5.
source_labels: Young-onset diabetes in Asian Indians linked to lower beta-cell function (INSPIRED; Diabetologia 2022)
overclaim_guard: Lean diabetes reflects reduced β-cell function / 'thin-fat' body composition, not the absence of metabolic risk; a normal BMI is not reassurance.
notes: INSPIRED study. Reinforces the BMI-23 diabetes-screening trigger.

sa_value: early_screen=first-trimester screening for high-risk groups including South Asian ancestry; iadpsg_75g_ogtt_cutoffs=FPG>=92, 1h>=180, 2h>=153 mg/dL (numeric cutoffs unchanged); guidance=SA women develop GDM at lower BMI and earlier in pregnancy; screen earlier, same thresholds.
source_labels: IADPSG recommendations on diagnosis & classification of hyperglycemia in pregnancy (Diabetes Care 2010) | Diagnosis and Classification of Diabetes: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2024
overclaim_guard: The diagnostic thresholds are unchanged; the SA-specific action is earlier screening, not a different cutoff.
notes: IADPSG 2010; ADA 2024 §15 (Pregnancy); FIGO 2015.

analyte: Age at natural menopause (South Asian women)
sa_value: median_age=49; early_or_premature_le45_pct=0.3; guidance=~30% of US SA women experience early/premature menopause (<=45y) vs <7% in other US groups.
source_labels: Menopausal experience (hot flashes, urinary incontinence, mood) among South Asian American women — MASALA (Lyu et al., Climacteric 2025)
overclaim_guard: Cohort estimate (n=405); earlier menopause raises cardiometabolic risk earlier but is not itself a disease.
notes: Lyu, Climacteric 2025. New domain: women's health.

sa_value: high_parity=>=2 live births; effect_over_5y_vs_0_1=SBP +8.9 mmHg, DBP +4.1 mmHg, LDL-C +8.9 mg/dL
source_labels: Association of Parity With ASCVD Risk Factors Among South Asian Women — MASALA (Mehta et al., JACC Advances 2026)
overclaim_guard: Association from MASALA (n~415); parity is a risk-context flag, not a screening threshold.
notes: Mehta, JACC Advances 2026. Surface on women's cardiometabolic panels.

sa_value: adjust=False; guidance=Do NOT treat MTHFR C677T as a South Asian homocysteine variant — it is not enriched in SA. Elevated homocysteine here is driven by dietary (vegetarian) B12 deficiency; treat the B12, not the SNP.
source_labels: Hyperhomocysteinemia & cobalamin (B12) deficiency in Asian Indians (Refsum et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2001)
overclaim_guard: Credibility-showcase 'do-not-apply' row: the actionable target is B12 status, not MTHFR genotyping.
notes: Driver evidence: Refsum 2001 (B12 deficiency → hyperhomocysteinemia in Indians).

sa_value: note=The ApoB/ApoA-I ratio was the strongest single modifiable MI predictor in South Asians (INTERHEART; population-attributable risk ~47%).; guidance=Where available, weight the ApoB/ApoA-I ratio over LDL-C alone for SA risk discussions.
source_labels: Risk factors for early MI in South Asians (INTERHEART, JAMA 2007)
overclaim_guard: No SA-specific numeric ratio cutoff is defined here; the claim is the ratio's predictive weight, not a threshold.
notes: Reuses the INTERHEART citation already in the dataset. PRAJNA-APOBA1.

sa_value: low=0.4; high=4.0; guidance=Reference range unchanged; the SA-relevant point is high hypothyroidism prevalence in Indian adults, supporting a lower threshold to check TSH.
source_labels: Prevalence of hypothyroidism in adults: epidemiological study in eight cities of India (Unnikrishnan et al., Indian J Endocrinol Metab 2013)
overclaim_guard: Not a redefined range — a prevalence-driven screening emphasis; do not present a different TSH normal range for SA.
notes: Unnikrishnan, Indian thyroid epidemiology. Soft/borderline candidate. TSH LOINC 3016-3.
